name: water_infrastructure_classroom_northpine_car_fair
premise: A cross-functional incident desk is managing a domain incident with incomplete information and high reputational exposure. Stakeholders disagree on what counts as verified fact versus prudent assumption. The situation maps to internal runbooks and external messaging tied to: water infrastructure classroom northpine car fair. The moral tension is explicitly framed as care versus fairness: leadership cannot fully satisfy both without accepting tradeoffs. Pager queues are saturated; middle management is forwarding screenshots from an internal channel that is already leaking. The breach-notification and customer-communication clock has started; executive staff expect a single coherent position before the next press cycle. Any exception must be time-boxed, named, and reversible where technically feasible. Treat unverified telemetry as hypothesis; do not assert facts you cannot support from retained logs.

agents.name: Zara Haddad
agents.identity: I am Zara Haddad, the accountable decision-maker on this Water Infrastructure bridge; I own what gets logged, communicated, and executed in the next window.
agents.goal: I need to stop the bleeding without baking in a rule that later punishes fairness whenever someone invokes care.

agents.name: Hector Dubois
agents.identity: I am Hector Dubois, responsible for surfacing who gets protected last when systems fail; I am watching for silent harm and unfair distribution.
agents.goal: I want safeguards so fairness is not sacrificed by default just because care feels morally loud in the moment.

choices: I will choose a narrow, time-boxed authorization with explicit human override, subgroup monitoring, and a hard stop if harm indicators rise—accepting slower relief to protect fairness.

I will choose a broad immediate authorization to stabilize outcomes fast, with transparent metrics and rapid correction afterward—accepting higher risk to fairness to secure care before the window closes.
forced_choice.call_to_action: External parties will infer a decision if none is posted. With partial evidence, Zara Haddad must decide under care versus fairness. What does Zara Haddad do?
